description: Integrated analysis of nocturnal pulse oximetry — oxygen saturation dynamics, heart rate variability proxies, desaturation profiling, sleep architecture proxies, and cross-signal intelligence from 1 Hz reflectance oximetry.
warning: All values are derived from consumer-grade 1 Hz reflectance oximetry. They are not equivalent to clinical polysomnography and do not constitute a medical diagnosis. HR-derived metrics labelled 'proxy' cannot be compared against published HRV clinical norms based on beat-to-beat RR intervals.

dataModel.description: Expected raw input from O2Ring or equivalent 1 Hz reflectance oximeter

sections.title: SpO2 Basics & ODI
sections.description: Oxygen saturation fundamentals and desaturation event counting

metrics.name: Mean SpO2
metrics.fullName: Mean peripheral oxygen saturation

metrics.name: Oxygen Desaturation Index (4%)
metrics.fullName: Events per hour where SpO2 drops ≥4% from 5-min rolling baseline
metrics.formula: events(SpO2 drop ≥4% from 5-min rolling baseline) / recording_hours
metrics.formulaNote: Rolling baseline = mean SpO2 over preceding 5 min. Event ends when SpO2 returns within 1% of baseline or 120s elapses. Min event 10s. Refractory 10s after recovery.

metrics.name: Oxygen Desaturation Index (3%)
metrics.fullName: Events per hour where SpO2 drops ≥3% from rolling baseline

metrics.name: Oxygen Desaturation Index (2%)
metrics.fullName: Events per hour where SpO2 drops ≥2% from rolling baseline

metrics.name: Oxygen Desaturation Index (1%)
metrics.fullName: Events per hour where SpO2 drops ≥1% from rolling baseline

sections.title: Thresholds & T-Index
sections.description: Cumulative time below SpO2 thresholds — the most clinically actionable saturation metrics

metrics.name: Time Below 95%
metrics.fullName: Percentage of recording where SpO2 < 95%

metrics.name: Time Below 90%
metrics.fullName: Percentage of recording where SpO2 < 90%

metrics.name: Cumulative Time Below 90%
metrics.fullName: Total seconds where SpO2 < 90%

metrics.name: Hypoxic Burden (Area Under Curve)
metrics.fullName: Integral of SpO2 deficit below 90%, normalized per hour

metrics.name: Time Below 88%
metrics.fullName: Percentage of recording where SpO2 < 88%

metrics.name: Cyclic Desaturation Index
metrics.fullName: Downward SpO2 crossings of recording mean per hour
metrics.formulaNote: Sensitive to rhythmic desaturation (Cheyne–Stokes, PB) even when individual dips are shallow.

metrics.name: Weighted Desaturation Severity Index
metrics.fullName: Quadratically weighted depth×duration severity
metrics.formulaNote: Penalizes deeper and longer events quadratically, reflecting non-linear physiological stress.

metrics.name: ODI Ratio Index
metrics.fullName: ODI-3 / ODI-1 ratio — pattern discriminator
metrics.formulaNote: Low ratio → shallow frequent events (CS/PB). High ratio → deep events (obstructive).

metrics.name: Delta SpO2 Instability Index
metrics.fullName: Mean absolute SpO2 difference 3s apart

metrics.name: Desaturation Severity
metrics.fullName: AHI surrogate (Kulkas 2013) — ODI-3 × mean depth × mean duration

metrics.name: % Recording with Desaturation Events
metrics.fullName: Fraction of recording inside ODI-3 events (Kulkas 2013)

metrics.name: Mean Oxygen Desaturation Level
metrics.fullName: Mean SpO2 of all samples inside desaturation events
metrics.formula: mean(SpO2[t] for t inside any event)

metrics.name: Mean Desaturation Rate
metrics.fullName: Mean rate of fall from baseline to nadir (%/s)
metrics.formulaNote: Steeper (more negative) = abrupt/obstructive. Gradual = central/hypoventilation.

metrics.name: Mean Resaturation Rate
metrics.fullName: Mean rate of recovery from nadir toward baseline (%/s)

metrics.name: Clustering Index
metrics.fullName: Fraction of nadirs in second half of recording
metrics.formulaNote: >0.6 suggests REM-concentrated or late-night clustering. <0.4 suggests early/onset.

sections.title: Advanced SpO2 Metrics
sections.description: Signal dynamics, periodicity, and nonlinear analysis

metrics.name: Detrended Fluctuation Analysis α1
metrics.fullName: Self-similarity of SpO2 fluctuations on short timescales (4–16s)
metrics.formula: slope of log F(n) vs log n (n=4–16s windows)
metrics.formulaNote: α1 ≈ 0.5 = random; ≈ 1.0 = long-range correlated (OSA/PB); >1.0 = non-stationary.

metrics.name: Sample Entropy
metrics.fullName: SpO2 signal regularity (m=2, r=0.2×SD)
metrics.formulaNote: Low SampEn = repetitive cyclic pattern. High SampEn = irregular (normal).

metrics.name: Dominant Frequency (DFT)
metrics.fullName: Strongest periodic component in SpO2 signal

metrics.name: Baseline Slope Across Night
metrics.fullName: OLS slope of 5-min rolling SpO2 baseline

metrics.name: Weighted Time-Area Hypoxic Load
metrics.fullName: Severity-weighted sum of time below SpO2 thresholds

metrics.name: Coefficient of Variation of SpO2
metrics.fullName: SD / mean × 100 for whole-night SpO2

metrics.name: Lag-1 Autocorrelation
metrics.fullName: Pearson correlation between consecutive SpO2 values

metrics.name: SpO2 Kurtosis
metrics.fullName: Tail weight of SpO2 distribution

metrics.name: SpO2 Skewness
metrics.fullName: Asymmetry of SpO2 distribution

metrics.name: Conditional Mean Below 94%
metrics.fullName: Average SpO2 during hypoxemic samples only

metrics.name: Interquartile Range of SpO2
metrics.fullName: p75 − p25 of whole-night SpO2

metrics.name: Inter-Event Interval
metrics.fullName: Mean and SD of time between consecutive nadirs

metrics.name: CV of Inter-Nadir Intervals
metrics.fullName: SD / mean × 100 of inter-nadir interval series

metrics.name: Desaturation Asymmetry
metrics.fullName: Dip–Recovery slope ratio
metrics.formulaNote: >1.5 = abrupt obstructive. <0.7 = gradual central/hypoventilation.

ranges.label: Gradual central — slow drop, fast rise
Abrupt obstructive — fast drop, slow rise

metrics.name: SpO2 Ceiling Runs
metrics.fullName: Count of ≥30s runs at exactly 100%
metrics.formula: count(runs where SpO2 == 100 for ≥30 consecutive seconds)
metrics.formulaNote: Signal quality flag — long runs at exactly 100% indicate firmware clamping or sensor issue.

metrics.name: O2-HR Efficiency
metrics.fullName: SpO2 recovery magnitude per unit cardiac work

compute.note: Requires per-event HR and SpO2 pairing

metrics.name: Post-Dip HR Response
metrics.fullName: Mean HR change 60s after nadir, averaged across events
metrics.formulaNote: Positive = preserved arousal coupling. Flat/negative = blunted autonomic arousal.

metrics.name: OxyCrash Rate
metrics.fullName: Acute SpO2 drops ≥5% in 30s, per hour
metrics.formulaNote: 30s refractory cooldown. Captures fast-onset desaturations outside ODI criteria.

metrics.name: AHI Estimate
metrics.fullName: Proxy AHI from ODI-4 (internal calibration; ODI-4 is a standard oximetry AHI surrogate)
metrics.formulaNote: Population-level proxy. ±5 events/hr uncertainty at individual level. Overestimates in CS/central patterns.

metrics.name: Worst 10-min Mean SpO2
metrics.fullName: Lowest mean SpO2 in any 10-minute sliding window

metrics.name: Worst 30-min T95
metrics.fullName: Highest T95 found in any 30-minute rolling window

metrics.name: Stable SpO2 Windows
metrics.fullName: Count of 5-min windows with SpO2 SD <1% and mean >94%

sections.title: Literature-Inspired Clinical Indices
sections.description: Indices derived from published methods — verify exact citations before clinical use

metrics.name: Sleep-disordered Breathing Intensity Index
metrics.fullName: Composite depth×duration index (Hui 2024, SHHS n=4485)
metrics.formulaNote: Depth squared reflects super-linear dose–response to CVD mortality. Q5 ~2.5× mortality vs Q1.

compute.note: Requires per-event extraction with squared depth

metrics.name: McGill-style Oximetry Grade
metrics.fullName: Custom adult oximetry severity grade (1–4)
metrics.formula: Scored from ODI-4 rate and CT<90
metrics.formulaNote: Not the published pediatric McGill Oximetry Score. Custom adult proxy.

sections.title: Hypoxic Burden Metrics
sections.description: Indices linking hypoxic exposure depth and duration to cardiovascular outcomes

metrics.name: Cumulative Hypoxemia Area Below 94%
metrics.fullName: Total area under SpO2 curve below fixed 94% line, per hour
metrics.formulaNote: Fixed-threshold integral — not the validated event-based Azarbarzin 2019 hypoxic burden.

metrics.name: Hypoxic Dose (94%)
metrics.fullName: Per-hour depth–duration product relative to 94% threshold (hypoxic-dose framework; Azarbarzin 2019/2020)

metrics.name: Hypoxic Load
metrics.fullName: Event-based approximation of Azarbarzin 2019 hypoxic burden
metrics.formulaNote: Approximation using triangular-area estimate, not literal per-event baseline method.

sections.title: Heart Rate Basics
sections.description: Nocturnal HR statistics from 1 Hz oximeter photoplethysmography

metrics.name: 5th-Percentile Still Heart Rate
metrics.fullName: 5th-percentile HR from motion-free samples

metrics.name: Linear HR Trend Across Night
metrics.fullName: OLS slope of HR vs time (bpm/hr)
metrics.formula: OLS(all motion-free HR samples vs. elapsed time)

metrics.name: Mean Still Heart Rate
metrics.fullName: Mean HR from motion-free samples

sections.title: HRV Proxy Metrics
sections.description: Variability estimates from 1 Hz HR — directional autonomic indicators only

metrics.name: SDNN Proxy
metrics.fullName: SD of motion-free HR values (1 Hz)
metrics.formulaNote: Analogous to clinical SDNN but numerically different. Higher = greater autonomic variability.

metrics.name: RMSSD Proxy
metrics.fullName: Root mean square of successive HR differences (1 Hz)
metrics.formulaNote: Sensitive to parasympathetic (vagal) tone. Higher = stronger nocturnal vagal activity.

metrics.name: pNN3 Equivalent
metrics.fullName: % of consecutive HR pairs differing by ≥3 bpm
metrics.formulaNote: Analogous to clinical pNN50, scaled for 1 Hz integer HR data.

metrics.name: RMSSD Trajectory Across Night
metrics.fullName: OLS slope of 30-min windowed RMSSD values
metrics.formula: OLS slope of RMSSD[window] vs. window_index
metrics.formulaNote: Rising arc = increasing vagal tone (good recovery). Flat/declining = impaired.

metrics.name: LF/HF Ratio
metrics.fullName: Low/High frequency HR band power ratio
metrics.formula: LF(0.04–0.15 Hz) / HF(0.15–0.40 Hz) band power
metrics.formulaNote: DFT-based on capped subsample ≤1800s. Proxy only — not suitable for clinical autonomic diagnosis.

metrics.name: Approximate Entropy
metrics.fullName: Nonlinear HR regularity measure (Pincus 1991)
metrics.formulaNote: Low = repetitive (autonomic suppression). High = complex (healthy variability). Corrected in v22.15.

metrics.name: Poincaré Short/Long-Term HRV
metrics.fullName: Geometric HRV from Poincaré plot (HR[t] vs HR[t+1])
metrics.formulaNote: SD1 = beat-to-beat variability (vagal). SD2 = long-term variability (total autonomic).

metrics.name: Respiratory Sinus Arrhythmia Proxy
metrics.fullName: SD of SpO2 within 30s motion-free windows, averaged

compute.note: Requires windowed SD with motion filter

metrics.name: Interquartile Range of HR
metrics.fullName: p75 − p25 of motion-free HR values

metrics.name: Heart Rate Coefficient of Variation
metrics.fullName: SD(HR) / mean(HR) × 100 for motion-free samples

metrics.name: Vagal Index
metrics.fullName: Composite vagal tone proxy from HR variability

sections.title: Spike Analysis
sections.description: Heart rate spike detection and autonomic arousal indexing

metrics.name: HR Spike Count
metrics.fullName: Count of HR elevations ≥10 bpm above 5-min rolling median

metrics.name: Autonomic Arousal Index
metrics.fullName: HR spikes per hour (arousal proxy)

sections.title: Sleep Timing Metrics
sections.description: Sleep onset, wake, and ultradian cycle detection

metrics.name: Sleep Onset Latency
metrics.fullName: Time from recording start to first sustained low-motion period

metrics.name: Wake After Sleep Onset
metrics.fullName: Total awake minutes after initial sleep onset

metrics.name: Ultradian Cycles
metrics.fullName: Estimated number of ~90-min sleep cycles
metrics.formula: count(HR trough clusters spaced 70–120 min apart)

sections.title: Sleep Stage Proxies
sections.description: REM and deep sleep estimates from HR and motion patterns

metrics.name: REM Proxy
metrics.fullName: Estimated REM minutes from HR elevation + low motion episodes
metrics.formula: count(motion=0 AND HR > rolling_p75 for ≥5 min blocks)

metrics.name: NREM Deep Proxy
metrics.fullName: Estimated deep sleep from low HR + low motion + stable SpO2
metrics.formula: count(motion=0 AND HR < p25 AND SpO2 SD < 1% for ≥5 min)

metrics.name: Sleep Stability Index
metrics.fullName: Fraction of night in continuous ≥15-min low-motion blocks

metrics.name: WASO Percentage
metrics.fullName: Wake time as percentage of total sleep period

metrics.name: Sleep Periodicity Index
metrics.fullName: Regularity of sleep–wake cycling across the night

metrics.name: Motion Percentage
metrics.fullName: Percentage of recording with motion detected

metrics.name: Nocturnal Stability Index
metrics.fullName: Composite 0–100 score integrating SpO2, HRV, motion, and event burden
metrics.formulaNote: 100 = perfectly stable night. Each sub-score normalized 0–100.

metrics.name: Cheyne-Stokes Score
metrics.fullName: Likelihood of periodic breathing pattern

metrics.name: Sleep Fragmentation Index
metrics.fullName: Composite of motion%, awakenings, and HR spike burden

metrics.name: Cardiorespiratory Coupling Index
metrics.fullName: Correlation between SpO2 oscillations and HR oscillations

metrics.name: SpO2–HR Lag
metrics.fullName: Temporal lag (s) of peak cross-correlation between SpO2 and HR

sections.title: VO2max Estimate
sections.description: Estimated maximal oxygen consumption from nocturnal HR data

metrics.name: Estimated VO2max
metrics.fullName: Estimated maximal oxygen consumption (Uth–Sørensen HR-ratio)
metrics.formulaNote: Uth–Sørensen HR-ratio formula. HRmax assumed 208 − 0.7×age (Tanaka 2001) if not directly measured. Proxy only.

compute.note: Requires age or HRmax input + HR_floor

sections.title: Karvonen Zones
sections.description: Heart rate training zones from nocturnal resting HR

metrics.name: Karvonen HR Zones
metrics.fullName: Five training zones from HR_floor and estimated HRmax

sections.title: Multi-Night Trends
sections.description: Longitudinal patterns across multiple recording nights

metrics.name: Multi-Night NSI Mean ± SD
metrics.fullName: Mean and standard deviation of NSI across recording nights

metrics.name: Inter-Night SpO2 Variation
metrics.fullName: CV of mean SpO2 across nights

metrics.name: ODI-4 Trend (first half vs second)
metrics.fullName: Change in ODI-4 between first and second half of recording period

metrics.name: Poor Nights Percentage
metrics.fullName: Percentage of nights with NSI < 50

sections.title: Signal Quality
sections.description: Data quality assessment and artifact detection

metrics.name: HR Artifact Percentage
metrics.fullName: Percentage of HR samples flagged as artifact
metrics.formulaNote: Artifact = HR outside 30–220 bpm or sudden >30 bpm jump in 1s.

ranges.label: Significant artifacts — results may be unreliable

metrics.name: HR Flatline Count
metrics.fullName: Count of runs where HR is identical for ≥10 consecutive seconds

metrics.name: Data Gap Count
metrics.fullName: Count of missing-data intervals ≥5 seconds

metrics.name: Longest Clean SpO2 Period
metrics.fullName: Longest continuous run with SpO2 SD < 1% and mean > 94%

compute.note: Requires run-length analysis on windowed stats

abbreviations.biCV: Coefficient of Variation of Inter-Nadir Intervals
abbreviations.pNN3: Percentage of successive HR differences ≥ 3 bpm
abbreviations.pNN50: Percentage of successive RR differences ≥ 50 ms
abbreviations.RMSSD: Root Mean Square of Successive Differences
